extraction.one_line_summary: GROVES Kaden won bunch sprint after early all-out racing on Tawonga Gap shattered field but breakaway absorbed on final climb.
tactical_patterns: POWER Robert sacrificed GC position to work for teammate HINDLEY, finishing 8:27 down reveals team prioritization over individual ambition.
Opening climb at maximum tempo despite 178km race length shows teams willing to burn domestiques early for positioning control.
Sunweb controlled gap at 4+ minutes then timed chase to absorb break exactly on final climb summit shows advanced pacing calculation.
RICE Michael refusing breakaway turns but still finishing 5th demonstrates free-riding sprint strategy can work if teammates don't attack.

rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: positive: first Mitchelton-Scott win shows he has speed to beat DAINESE Alberto when positioned correctly by leadout
rider_signals.DAINESE Alberto: negative: dropped on final climb summit despite stage 1 win reveals vulnerability on even modest gradients before sprints
rider_signals.POWER Robert: negative: 8:27 time loss while working for HINDLEY shows complete subordination of personal GC for team leader support
rider_signals.HINDLEY Jai: positive: trouble-free day maintaining yellow suggests climbing strength without needing to defend actively yet
rider_signals.RIES Michel: neutral: fifth place after refusing all breakaway work shows parasitic tactics can yield results without team consequences
